德乙漢諾威
Timo Hubers同Jannes Horn分別12/3同13/3確診武漢肺炎, 整隊漢諾威要隔離14日

【武漢肺炎】隊報：歐洲國家盃順延至2021年舉行 ｜ 體嘢報道

閱讀全文：https://wp.me/p75t7v-85g

根據法國《隊報》的消息，歐洲足協將下週二和55個成員國代表的會議上，宣布暫停今季歐聯、歐霸賽事，而原訂於今年6月舉行的歐洲國家盃則會被推遲至2021年。

未官方

據英國《BBC》報道，在聯賽延期後，阿仙奴的球員及職員皆沒有接受武肺病毒測試。該報道指，目前只有不足五名的阿仙奴球員正在處於自我隔離狀態，原因是在對陣奧林比亞高斯的歐霸盃賽事中，這些球員曾在球員通道中與馬連基斯接觸過，另外有部份阿仙奴職員亦需要進行隔離。據指阿仙奴方面已經對阿聯酋球場進行全面清潔，只不過一眾隔離的槍手球員及職員並沒有接受病毒測試。

阿仙奴球員的14天隔離時間將會在周四結束，球隊在周六作客白禮頓的聯賽將會如常舉行，至於未有受影響的球員就已經在周二繼續正常接受訓練。

https://hk.appledaily.com/sports/20200312/RL75R4HZ64EOM56HRAKPNPUMYI/

Watford FC

First Team 23 minutes ago
Pearson: “We Are Talking About Sport Versus Well-Being”

By Kevin Affleck

Nigel Pearson said he cancelled training yesterday after several members of his squad reported feeling unwell this week.

The Head Coach said the preparations for the Premier League game against Leicester have been disrupted because of the health of some of his players, one of whom is awaiting results of a test following symptoms associated with coronavirus.

He said that the priority remains the “interests of the people who work here and their wider families”. “We are talking about sport versus the well-being of people here,” he added.

繼加比亞甸尼後，不幸地森多利亞再有五人確診武漢肺炎，包括一名Medical Director Amedeo Baldari和四名球員，中場主力瑞典國腳伊歷度Albin Ekdal, 罔比亞中堅高利Omar Colley, , 本土前鋒拉古文拿Antonino La Gumina, 與及挪威中場科斯比Morten Thorsby。

其中伊歷度跟加比亞甸尼一樣在週日對維羅納一役正選上陣，而科斯比則後備登場。

Swiss soccer president Blanc tests positive for coronavirus

BERN, Switzerland (AP) — The president of Switzerland’s soccer federation has tested positive for the coronavirus.

The Swiss federation said 70-year-old Dominique Blanc got the results of a test on Sunday morning and isolated himself at his home.

“I feel pretty good right now and have only mild flu symptoms,” Blanc said in a statement released by the federation. He was tested after developing a sore throat and mild cough.

The headquarters of Swiss soccer in Bern has been closed and staff who had recent contact with Blanc have received medical advice, the federation said.

Blanc attended the annual meeting of European soccer body UEFA on March 3, and raised the issue of consequences for shutting down domestic soccer with leaders from 55 member countries.

“Due to the coronavirus we are in a situation that could shake, for a part of us, our professional football to its foundations,” Blanc said in Amsterdam.

The day before Blanc spoke, Switzerland was among the first countries in Europe to suspend its national league due to the spreading risk of COVID-19.

Switzerland’s soccer federation will take part in conference call talks with UEFA on Tuesday to discuss plans for how or if to restart the European soccer season which is now in almost total shutdown.

replies.msg:
Albion clarify Austin “virus” story

Albion would like to clarify that striker Charlie Austin has NOT tested positive for COVID-19.

Charlie reported symptoms commonly associated with the coronavirus and on medical advice from the Club was told to stay away from training and “self-isolate” a week ago.

Contrary to reports this evening, Charlie was not tested for the virus and has subsequently made a full recovery.


“I felt terrible for a few days but I never tested for the virus and feel fine now,” said Charlie.

“It was sensible advice to self-isolate from the rest of the lads and from my family during this period. I’ve now made a full recovery.”

The Club would like to confirm that no other players have required testing for coronavirus.

https://www.wba.co.uk/news/2020/march/albion-clarify-austin-virus-story/
